2603.17952.title: Gender Disambiguation in Machine Translation: Diagnostic Evaluation in Decoder-Only Architectures
2603.17952.paper_summary: While Large Language Models achieve state-of-the-art results across a wide range of NLP tasks, they remain prone to systematic biases. Among these, gender bias is particularly salient in MT, due to systematic differences across languages in whether and how gender is marked. As a result, translation often requires disambiguating implicit source signals into explicit gender-marked forms. In this context, standard benchmarks may capture broad disparities but fail to reflect the full complexity of gender bias in modern MT. In this paper, we extend recent frameworks on bias evaluation by: (i) introducing a novel measure coined "Prior Bias", capturing a model's default gender assumptions, and (ii) applying the framework to decoder-only MT models. Our results show that, despite their scale and state-of-the-art status, decoder-only models do not generally outperform encoder-decoder architectures on gender-specific metrics; however, post-training (e.g., instruction tuning) not only improves contextual awareness but also reduces the masculine Prior Bias.
2603.17952.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型在廣泛的自然語言處理任務中達到了最先進的結果，但它們仍然容易受到系統性偏見的影響。<lb/>在這些偏見中，性別偏見在機器翻譯中特別明顯，因為不同語言在性別的標記方式和是否標記上存在系統性差異。<lb/>因此，翻譯通常需要將隱含的源信號釐清為明確的性別標記形式。<lb/>在這種情況下，標準基準可能會捕捉到廣泛的差異，但未能反映現代機器翻譯中性別偏見的全部複雜性。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過以下方式擴展了最近的偏見評估框架： (i) 引入一種新穎的度量，稱為「先驗偏見」，捕捉模型的預設性別假設， (ii) 將該框架應用於僅解碼器的機器翻譯模型。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，儘管它們的規模和最先進的地位，僅解碼器模型在性別特定指標上通常不如編碼-解碼架構；然而，後訓練（例如，指令調整）不僅提高了上下文意識，還減少了男性化的先驗偏見。
2603.17952.author: Chiara Manna et.al.
2603.17952.authors: Chiara Manna, Hosein Mohebbi, Afra Alishahi, Frédéric Blain, Eva Vanmassenhove
